sections.title: 0. Setup: invariants of the (t = 4, k = (5,5,5,5)) residual

tests.name: V_piece = 8/15
t = 4 (each piece touches all 4 T-faces)
k = (5, 5, 5, 5) (each T-face touched by all 5 pieces)

sections.title: 1. Convex polytope face-count: each piece has f >= 4

tests.name: f(P) >= 4 for any convex 3-polytope
With t = 4, P needs >= 4 boundary faces, so f(P) >= 4

sections.title: 2. f = 4 ELIMINATION: tetrahedral piece with all faces on dT

tests.name: f = 4 contradicts face-to-face property (0 interior faces, but pieces meet on positive 2-area)
tests.detail: by support hyperplane theorem + pieces tile T

tests.name: Conclusion: f(P) >= 5 for the (t=4) residual
f = 5 -> interior face-instances = 5
5 / 2 is not an integer -> face-to-face PARITY VIOLATION
Conclusion: f(P) >= 6 with f - 4 even

sections.title: 4. f = 6 structure: piece = T cap H_1 cap H_2

tests.name: f = 6: total interior face-instances = 10
f = 6: 10 / 2 = 5 distinct interior 2-faces
f = 6 with all triangular faces -> v = 5, e = 9 (Euler: 5 - 9 + 6 = 2)

sections.title: 5. Boundary-area equipartition: each footprint area = 2 sqrt 3 / 5

tests.name: Per-piece-per-T-face footprint area squared = 12 / 25
Sum over T-faces of piece p's footprint area = 4 * (2 sqrt 3 / 5) = 8 sqrt 3 / 5 = E

sections.title: 6. T-vertex Coxeter budget under Hypothesis H

tests.name: m = 0: sum k_v = 0, but each k_v >= 1 forbids this -> m = 0 IMPOSSIBLE
tests.detail: no piece touches any T-vertex, but T-vertices are on dT -- contradiction

tests.name: m = 1: sum k_v = 5; feasible ordered tuples = 0
m = 2: sum k_v = 10; feasible ordered tuples = 4
m = 3: sum k_v = 15; feasible ordered tuples = 8
m = 4: sum k_v = 20; feasible ordered tuples = 30
m = 1: NO Hypothesis-H Coxeter 4-tuple sums to 5 -> m = 1 IMPOSSIBLE under H

sections.title: 7. Centroid pyramid decomposition: V_piece = (a / 3) * h_T forces f = 4

tests.name: V_T = (A_T / 3) * h_T  (squared: V_T^2 = (A_T / 3)^2 * h_T^2)
Boundary contribution squared (a^2 * h_T^2) / 9 = V_piece^2 = 64 / 225
EQUALITY V_piece = (1/3) * a * h_T forces interior-facet contribution = 0
tests.detail: centroid pyramid decomposition with strict-interior apex p has all positive distances

tests.name: Interior-facet contribution = 0 forces f(P) = 4 (no interior facets)
tests.detail: but Section 2 rules out f = 4 by face-to-face property

tests.name: CONTRADICTION: V_piece = 8/15 is NOT achievable by any convex P with f >= 5
tests.detail: saturation of pyramid bound + strict positivity of interior distances

tests.name: (t = 4, k = (5,5,5,5)) residual: UNCONDITIONALLY UNSAT
tests.detail: via Sections 2 (f=4), 3 (f=5), and 7 (f>=6) -- no admissible f

unsat_chain: S2: f = 4 forces 0 interior facets, violating face-to-face property
S3: f = 5 forces 5/2 paired interior facets (parity violation)
S7: f >= 6 violates V_piece = (1/3) * a * h_T equality (centroid pyramid decomposition: interior-facet pyramids have strictly positive volume, contradicting V_piece = 8/15)
